title: Core Platform Overview
content: PipelineAI is an AI-powered sales engagement platform that automates personalized outbound at scale. It helps sales development teams generate 3x more qualified pipeline without adding headcount. Core capabilities: AI-generated personalized email sequences, multi-channel outreach orchestration (email, LinkedIn, phone), real-time buying intent signals, and automated CRM enrichment. Integrates natively with Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, and Slack.

title: Pricing Overview
content: Three tiers: Starter at $79/user/month for teams up to 5 (1,000 AI-generated emails/month, basic sequences, email only). Growth at $149/user/month for teams up to 25 (5,000 AI emails/month, multi-channel, intent signals, A/B testing). Enterprise at custom pricing for 25+ users (unlimited AI emails, dedicated CSM, custom integrations, SLA). All plans include 14-day free trial, no credit card required. Annual billing saves 20%.

title: Integration Capabilities
content: Native integrations: CRM (Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive), Email (Gmail, Outlook 365), Communication (Slack, Teams), Data (Snowflake, BigQuery), Sales tools (Outreach, SalesLoft — for migration). REST API available for custom integrations. Typical setup: 2-4 hours for CRM + email. Dedicated integrations engineer included on Growth and Enterprise plans. SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant, data encrypted at rest and in transit.

title: Key Differentiators
content: Three things set PipelineAI apart: 1) AI personalization that actually works — our model researches each prospect and writes messaging that references their specific context, not just merge fields. Average reply rates are 3.2x higher than template-based tools. 2) Intent signal engine — we surface which prospects are actively researching solutions like yours, so your team focuses on warm leads, not cold lists. 3) Unified multi-channel — email, LinkedIn, and phone sequences in one workflow with AI deciding the optimal channel and timing per prospect.

title: Platform Results & Metrics
content: Average customer results after 90 days: 3.2x increase in email reply rates, 2.4x more qualified meetings booked per SDR, 45% reduction in time spent on manual research and personalization, 60% improvement in pipeline velocity. These are averages across our customer base of 400+ B2B companies. Results vary by industry and team size, but directionally consistent.

title: Onboarding & Time to Value
content: Typical onboarding timeline: Day 1 — CRM and email connected, team accounts created. Days 2-3 — AI model trained on your product, ICP, and messaging tone. Days 4-5 — first AI-generated sequences reviewed and launched. Week 2 — first replies coming in. Most teams see measurable pipeline impact within 30 days. Dedicated onboarding specialist for Growth and Enterprise. Self-serve setup for Starter with video guides and live chat support.

title: Too Expensive
content: Acknowledge: I hear you — budget matters, especially in this market. Reframe: What teams typically find is that PipelineAI pays for itself within 60 days. Think about it this way — if each SDR books even 3 extra meetings a month from better personalization, and your average deal size is over $10k, the math works out fast. Evidence: CloudMetrics, a 30-person SaaS company, was spending $12k/month on manual outbound efforts across tools and contractor time. They consolidated to PipelineAI at $4k/month and actually increased output by 2x. Question: What are you spending today across your outbound tools and processes? That usually helps us figure out if the economics make sense.

title: Not the Right Time
content: Acknowledge: Totally fair — timing is everything and I don't want to waste your time. Reframe: What I've seen is that pipeline gaps tend to compound. Teams that wait a quarter to fix outbound usually spend the next quarter trying to catch up. The setup is lightweight — we're talking days, not months. Evidence: DataStack told us the same thing in March. They started in May and their VP Sales said he wished they'd moved in January because they missed a full quarter of pipeline. Question: What would need to change for the timing to feel right? Is it a resource thing, a priority thing, or something else?

title: Already Using Outreach
content: Acknowledge: Outreach is solid — they've built a strong platform for sequence management. Reframe: What we hear from teams who've used both is that Outreach is great for running sequences, but the personalization ceiling is low. You're still writing templates and using merge fields. PipelineAI's AI actually researches each prospect and generates unique messaging. Evidence: RevOps Solutions had 8 SDRs on Outreach and was averaging a 2.1% reply rate. After switching to PipelineAI, they hit 6.8% within 60 days — same list, same team, just better messaging. Question: How are your reply rates looking on Outreach right now? Are you happy with the personalization you're getting?

title: Already Using SalesLoft
content: Acknowledge: SalesLoft is a well-known platform — lots of teams have built their workflow around it. Reframe: Where we see teams outgrow SalesLoft is on the intelligence side. SalesLoft is cadence-driven — you set it and forget it. PipelineAI adapts in real-time: it reads engagement signals and adjusts messaging, channel, and timing per prospect automatically. Evidence: TechBridge had 12 reps on SalesLoft and switched to PipelineAI. Their meeting-booked rate went from 1.8% to 5.2% because the AI was optimizing what wasn't working mid-sequence instead of waiting for the cadence to finish. Question: Are your reps spending a lot of time manually tweaking sequences based on what's working?

title: Already Using Apollo
content: Acknowledge: Apollo is great for prospecting and building lists — really solid data. Reframe: Apollo's strength is the database and the basic sequencing. Where teams hit a wall is on the quality of outreach. Apollo sequences are template-based with merge fields. PipelineAI takes the prospect data — from Apollo or anywhere — and generates truly personalized messaging that references their specific situation. Some of our customers actually use Apollo for data and PipelineAI for engagement. Evidence: GrowthLabs was on Apollo's full platform and moved their engagement layer to PipelineAI. Same prospect lists, reply rates jumped from 1.5% to 4.9%. Question: Are you using Apollo mainly for data, engagement, or both?

title: I'm Not the Decision Maker
content: Acknowledge: Totally understand — these decisions usually involve a few people. Reframe: What I've found works well is if I can share enough context with you first so that when you bring it up internally, you have everything you need to make the case. A lot of our champions started exactly where you are. Question: Who else would typically be involved in evaluating a tool like this? Would it make sense to set up a quick 15-minute call with them included so everyone hears the same thing?

title: We Don't Need This / We're Fine
content: Acknowledge: Appreciate you being straight with me. Reframe: A lot of the teams we work with said the same thing — they had a process that was working okay. The shift happened when they realized how much time their SDRs were spending on research and personalization that the AI could handle in seconds. They weren't broken, they were just leaving efficiency on the table. Evidence: NexGen Security's VP Sales told us 'we're fine' on our first call. Two months later he came back because he realized his team was spending 3 hours per day per rep on manual prospect research. Question: How much time are your reps spending on research and writing emails versus actually selling?

title: Just Send Me an Email
content: Acknowledge: Happy to send something over. Reframe: I want to make sure I send you something actually relevant and not just a generic overview deck that ends up buried. Question: Quick question so I can tailor it — are you currently running outbound with a team, or is that something you're building out? That way I can include the right case studies and numbers.

title: We'll Build It Internally
content: Acknowledge: Makes sense — if you have the engineering resources, building gives you full control. Reframe: What we've seen is that teams who go the build route typically underestimate the maintenance cost. The AI models need continuous training, prompt tuning, and the deliverability infrastructure alone — warming, rotation, reputation management — is a full-time job. One of our customers, StackPulse, spent 6 months and about $200k trying to build their own before switching to PipelineAI. They said the opportunity cost of having engineers on this instead of their core product was the real killer. Question: Do you have a sense of the timeline and team you'd allocate to building it?

title: We're Under Contract with Another Tool
content: Acknowledge: Got it — no one likes paying for two tools at once. Reframe: A couple of options depending on your situation. We offer a migration credit that covers overlap periods for teams switching from Outreach, SalesLoft, or Apollo — typically 2-3 months. Also, some teams run PipelineAI in parallel during the last months of their contract to validate results before fully switching. Question: When does your current contract come up for renewal? Even if it's a few months out, it might be worth seeing the comparison now so you're ready when the time comes.

title: Outreach Battlecard
content: Outreach strengths: Market leader in sales engagement, strong sequence builder, good reporting, large integration ecosystem. Known weaknesses: Personalization is template + merge field based — no AI-generated messaging. Expensive at scale ($100-150/user/month on Pro). Complex setup and admin overhead. Common switching triggers: Low reply rates despite high send volume, reps spending too much time writing emails, leadership wanting AI-powered outreach. Our advantage: AI personalization that writes unique emails per prospect (not templates), 3.2x higher reply rates on average. Migration credit available. Do NOT say: Don't claim Outreach is outdated or bad — it's a solid tool, we just take personalization further.

title: SalesLoft Battlecard
content: SalesLoft strengths: Strong cadence management, good Salesforce integration, solid dialer, acquired by Vista Equity. Known weaknesses: Cadence-driven approach is rigid — doesn't adapt to engagement signals. AI features are surface-level (subject line suggestions, not full message generation). Reporting less granular than Outreach. Common switching triggers: Reps running the same cadences for months without optimizing, leadership wanting more intelligent outbound. Our advantage: Real-time sequence adaptation — PipelineAI adjusts messaging, channel, and timing per prospect based on engagement. Dynamic, not static. Do NOT say: Don't speculate about Vista Equity acquisition impact on product roadmap.

title: Apollo.io Battlecard
content: Apollo strengths: Massive B2B contact database (270M+), affordable entry point, combined prospecting + engagement in one tool. Good for startups and SMBs getting started with outbound. Known weaknesses: Engagement features are basic — template sequences, limited AI, no real personalization engine. Data quality can be inconsistent on smaller companies. Deliverability tooling is limited. Common switching triggers: Teams that outgrow basic sequencing and need higher reply rates, data quality issues on their target accounts. Our advantage: PipelineAI can actually work alongside Apollo — use Apollo for data, PipelineAI for engagement. Or full replacement with superior AI messaging. Do NOT say: Don't attack Apollo's data quality directly — say 'we integrate with multiple data sources' instead.

title: Instantly.ai Battlecard
content: Instantly strengths: Very affordable ($30-80/month), unlimited email accounts, good email warmup, popular with agencies and solo operators. Known weaknesses: No real AI personalization — just templates with variables. No multi-channel (email only). No CRM integration on lower tiers. Built for volume, not quality. Common switching triggers: Deliverability issues from high-volume sending, low reply rates despite sending thousands of emails, teams that need multi-channel and CRM integration. Our advantage: Quality over quantity approach. PipelineAI sends fewer, better emails that actually get replies. Multi-channel built in. Enterprise-grade deliverability. Do NOT say: Don't position Instantly as 'for beginners' — many sophisticated teams use it. Focus on the AI personalization gap.

title: CloudMetrics — SaaS SMB, Pipeline Growth
content: CloudMetrics, a 30-person SaaS company selling analytics tools to mid-market. Challenge: 4 SDRs sending 200+ emails/day with 1.8% reply rate. Spending $12k/month across Outreach licenses, a part-time contractor for research, and ZoomInfo. Solution: Switched to PipelineAI Growth plan. Results after 90 days: Reply rate jumped from 1.8% to 5.4%. Meetings booked per SDR went from 8/month to 22/month. Consolidated tech spend from $12k to $4k/month. Quote from VP Sales: 'The AI writes better cold emails than our best SDR. That's not an insult to the team — it just has more time to research each prospect.' They've since expanded to 8 SDRs on PipelineAI.

title: PayBridge — Fintech Mid-Market, Efficiency Gains
content: PayBridge, a 150-person fintech company selling payment infrastructure to e-commerce platforms. Challenge: 12-person SDR team using SalesLoft, averaging 6 meetings/month per rep. Reps spent 2.5 hours/day on prospect research. High turnover because the work felt repetitive. Solution: Implemented PipelineAI Enterprise. Results after 90 days: Meetings per SDR increased from 6 to 15/month. Research time dropped from 2.5 hours to 30 minutes/day. SDR turnover decreased because the role became more strategic — reps focused on conversations, not copy-pasting. Pipeline increased 2.8x with the same headcount. Quote from Head of Revenue: 'We were about to hire 6 more SDRs. PipelineAI made our existing 12 perform like 30.'

title: NexGen Security — Cybersecurity Enterprise, Competitive Displacement
content: NexGen Security, a 400-person cybersecurity company selling to enterprise IT teams. Challenge: Complex, technical sale. Generic outbound was getting ignored by CISOs and security engineers. 20-person SDR team on Outreach with 0.9% reply rate. Solution: PipelineAI Enterprise with custom AI model trained on cybersecurity terminology and buyer personas. Results after 120 days: Reply rate increased from 0.9% to 3.8%. The AI referenced specific CVEs, compliance frameworks, and tech stack details in each email. Enterprise meetings increased 4x. Average deal size actually increased because better-qualified meetings meant fewer tire-kickers. Quote from CRO: 'Our SDRs were sending emails that sounded like they were written by someone who doesn't understand security. PipelineAI changed that overnight.'

title: GrowthLabs — MarTech SMB, Apollo Migration
content: GrowthLabs, a 20-person marketing technology startup selling to marketing agencies. Challenge: Running Apollo for both data and engagement. Getting 1.5% reply rates on sequences. Deliverability issues because of high volume sending through Apollo. Solution: Kept Apollo for prospect data, moved engagement to PipelineAI Growth plan. Results after 60 days: Reply rates jumped from 1.5% to 4.9%. Deliverability improved because PipelineAI's sending infrastructure handles warmup and rotation. Total cost only increased by $800/month but meetings doubled. Quote from founder: 'Apollo is great for finding prospects. PipelineAI is great for actually getting them to respond. Using both was the unlock.'

title: Budget Discovery Questions
content: Do you have a budget set aside for sales engagement tools, or would this be a new line item? | What are you currently spending across your outbound stack — tools, data, contractors? | Is there a procurement or approval process we'd need to go through, or can your team evaluate independently?

title: Authority Discovery Questions
content: Who else would be involved in evaluating a tool like this? | What does the decision-making process typically look like for sales tools at your company? | Would it make sense to loop in your [VP Sales / CRO / Head of Revenue] for the demo so everyone hears the same thing?

title: Need Discovery Questions
content: How are you currently running outbound — is it all manual, or do you have tooling in place? | What's the biggest bottleneck for your SDR team right now? | If you could change one thing about how your team generates pipeline, what would it be? | How many meetings is each rep booking per month, and where does that need to be?

title: Timeline Discovery Questions
content: Is improving outbound something you're looking to address this quarter, or more of a next-half initiative? | Is there a revenue target or pipeline gap driving urgency here? | Are there any deadlines — like a board meeting or planning cycle — that make this more time-sensitive?

title: Sales Call Disclosure
content: If asked whether this is a sales call: 'Yes, this is a sales call. I'm reaching out because I think PipelineAI could help your team based on what I've seen about your outbound efforts.' Never deny this is a sales call. Never misrepresent the purpose of the call.

title: Do Not Call Request
content: If the prospect says 'stop calling', 'do not call me', 'take me off your list', or any similar request: immediately comply. Say: 'Absolutely, I'll make sure you're removed from our list right away. Apologies for the inconvenience, and have a good day.' Then end the call. Set call_outcome to 'do_not_call' in metadata. This is non-negotiable — do not try to persuade them to stay on the line.

title: No Unverified Claims
content: Never guarantee specific results. Always use qualifying language: 'on average', 'typically', 'customers report', 'based on what we've seen'. Never say 'you WILL get 3x pipeline' — say 'teams typically see around 2-3x pipeline improvement'. Never make claims about competitors that aren't in the competitor intelligence provided. If asked about a competitor you don't have data on, say 'I'm not super familiar with their latest features — I don't want to misrepresent them. What I can tell you is what PipelineAI does well.'
